control/subscribe.description: Subscribe to event channels. Filters vary by channel.

filters.description: Optional filters (chat-events: speaker, type, whisperOnly, userId; hooks: hookFilters; actor-events: actorUuid; scene-events: sceneId)

actorUuid.description: Filter actor-events to a specific actor UUID

hookFilters.description: Filter hooks channel to specific hook names (empty = all)

sceneId.description: Filter scene-events to a specific scene ID

speaker.description: Filter chat events by speaker alias

type.description: Filter chat events by message type

userId.description: Filter by Foundry user ID or username

channel.description: Omit to unsubscribe from all channels

events/actor.description: Actor update events from Foundry. Subscribe with { type: "subscribe", channel: "actor-events" }. Optionally filter by actorUuid.

event.description: Actor event type (e.g. actor-update, actor-create, actor-delete)

events/chat.description: Real-time chat message events from Foundry. Subscribe with { type: "subscribe", channel: "chat-events" }.

events/combat.description: Combat/encounter events from Foundry. Subscribe with { type: "subscribe", channel: "combat-events" }.

event.description: Combat event type (e.g. combat-start, combat-turn, combat-round, combat-end)

events/hooks.description: Generic Foundry hook events. Subscribe with { type: "subscribe", channel: "hooks" }. Use hookFilters to limit to specific hooks.

events/roll.description: Real-time dice roll events from Foundry. Subscribe with { type: "subscribe", channel: "roll-events" }.

events/scene.description: Scene events from Foundry. Subscribe with { type: "subscribe", channel: "scene-events" }. Optionally filter by sceneId.

message.name: ability-check

createChatMessage.description: Whether to post the roll to chat (default: true)

userId.description: Foundry user ID or username to scope permissions (omit for GM-level access)

session/interactive-input.description: Send input to an active interactive session

session/interactive-start.description: Start an interactive session with a Foundry entity (e.g. character sheet). The session allows sending input and receiving real-time updates.

uuid.description: UUID of the Foundry document to interact with

ws/remote-request.description: Invoke any supported action on a remote Foundry world via the relay tunnel The source connection token must list the target in allowedTargetClients and hold the required scope in remoteScopes. Configure these in the dashboard → Connections → Edit browser.

message.name: remote-request

action.description: Action to invoke on the target (e.g. create-user, entity, search)

autoStartIfOffline.description: Start a headless session if the target is offline (requires autoStartOnRemoteRequest enabled for that world)

payload.description: Action payload forwarded verbatim to the target module

targetClientId.description: Client ID of the target world (must be in allowedTargetClients)

message.summary: Invoke any supported action on a remote Foundry world via the relay tunnel

data.description: Response data from the target module (present on success)

error.description: Error message if the action failed or was rejected

info.description: Client-facing WebSocket API for bidirectional communication with Foundry VTT through the relay server. Connect to /ws/api with a token and clientId to send requests and receive real-time events.
info.title: FoundryVTT WebSocket API

clientId.description: ID of the connected Foundry instance to target
